metadata.description: Queue (FIFO): The `queue.Queue(maxsize)` is a first-in-first-out (FIFO) data structure. It provides methods such as `put(blocking)` to enqueue an item and `get(blocking)` to dequeue and retrieve an item. Additionally, there are `task_done()` and `join()` methods for signaling the completion of tasks and waiting until all tasks are done. Here is a brief Python code example: ```python import queue import threading def worker(q): while True: item = q.get() # Get an item from the queue if item is None: # Signal to stop break print(f"Processing {item}") q.task_done() # Indicate that a formerly enqueued task is complete q = queue.Queue(maxsize=10) # Create a queue with max size of 10 # Start worker threads for i in range(5): threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(q,), daemon=True).start() # Enqueue items for item in range(20): q.put(item) # Wait until all tasks are done q.join() ``` In this example: - `put(blocking)` is used to enqueue items. - `get(blocking)` retrieves an item from the queue. - `task_done()` signals that a task has been completed. - `join()` waits for all items in the queue to be processed.
